tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low HbA1c excludes chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
